summary: Close the arc-incidence lemma via self-affine fractal dimension: prove c_k(q,r) ≤ C·r^d on [1,10] with d≈1.10, C≈2 (availability-grade)

routes.route: ROUTE 1 — AFFINITY DIMENSION / SINGULAR-VALUE FUNCTION (Falconer). Treat Walk_{k+1}=M·Walk_k ⊔ Stitch_k as a quasi-self-affine curve; bound its box dimension by the affinity dimension of M's singular value function φ^s, and convert the box-covering count into the local incidence bound c_k(q,r) ≤ C·r^d. The decisive move: replace the isotropic Chebyshev pullback ‖M⁻¹‖∞=4/9 (which made every prior linear induction DIVERGE, coeff 20/9>1) by the anisotropic singular-value pullback φ = σ₁(M⁻¹)·σ₂(M⁻¹)^{d-1}, which CONTRACTS (0.353<1).
routes.proof:
Three rigorous ingredients + one residual clean lemma.

(1) BOUNDED DISTORTION (fully proven, linear algebra). M=((3,0,0),(0,0,-3),(0,3,-1)) decouples coord-0 (eigenvalue 3) from the 2×2 block ((0,-3),(3,-1)) with trace −1, det 9, hence eigenvalues (−1±i√35)/2 of modulus √9=3. All three eigenvalues have modulus 3=det(M)^{1/3}; M is diagonalizable ⇒ ELLIPTIC. I solved the invariant positive-definite form Q with MᵀQM=9Q (eig(Q)=[2.0,2.5,3.5]); in the Q-metric M=3·O with O orthogonal. Consequence: σ_i(M^j) ∈ [3^j/κ, κ·3^j] for ALL j, with κ=√(cond Q)=1.323. Verified: measured σ_i(M^j)/3^j ∈ [0.846,1.182] for j=1..10. So M^j has bounded distortion at every scale — the curve is quasi-self-SIMILAR with ratio 3, not genuinely anisotropic-self-affine.

(2) AFFINITY DIMENSION COLLAPSES TO THE SIMILARITY DIMENSION. Falconer's pressure equation for the λ branch-words of length n is Σ φ^s(M⁻ⁿ)=1 with φ^s=σ₁σ₂^{s-1} (1≤s≤2). By bounded distortion φ^s(M⁻ⁿ)=3^{-n}·3^{-n(s-1)}·(κ-factor)=3^{-ns}·O(1). Summing λⁿ words: λⁿ·3^{-ns}=O(1) ⇒ s* = log λ/log 3. The affinity dimension equals the similarity dimension d=log λ/log 3; distortion κ is absorbed into the covering CONSTANT, never the exponent.

(3) ANISOTROPIC CONTRACTION (the payoff that unblocks the induction). The crowding recursion c_{k+1}(q,r) = #{Walk_k ∩ M⁻¹B(q,r)} + refill. M⁻¹B(q,r) is an ELLIPSOID with semiaxes r·σ_i(M⁻¹)=(0.3935r,0.3333r,0.2824r). Covering a d-regular curve inside it takes ≍ σ₁·σ₂^{d-1}·r^d = φ·r^d boxes with φ=0.3935·0.3333^{0.10}=0.353<1. So under the ansatz c_k(·,·)≤C·(anisotropic content), the anchor term contributes ≤0.353·C·r^d and the induction CONTRACTS to a finite fixed point — exactly where the isotropic linear framing (4/9)(1+g)=20/9>1 diverged. This is the ingredient the refill-sublemma and route-4 gaps both flagged as "the honest path is the anisotropic M⁻¹ pullback."

RESIDUAL (clean lemma, below): d-regularity of ONE level's refill (anisotropic content bound / arc-incidence). Route 1 supplies (1)+(2)+(3) rigorously and reduces c_k≤C·r^d to this single-level statement, uniform in k by construction-homogeneity.
routes.d_derived: d = log λ / log 3, where 3 = det(M)^{1/3} = the exact Chebyshev-extent growth per level (measured extent_ratio = 3.0000/3.0002/3.0000 at L6/L7/L8) and λ = point-growth N_{k+1}/N_k. Bounded distortion (M elliptic, κ=1.323) makes Falconer's affinity dimension equal this similarity dimension, so d is an EXPONENT identity, not a fit. RIGOROUS BRACKET: 1 < d ≤ log 5/log 3 = 1.4650, the upper bound from the structural cap λ≤5 (≤4 stitch interiors per anchor-edge ⇒ N_{k+1}≤5N_k). MEASURED: λ_avg=3.355 (level-stable 3.348→3.362 across L6→L8) ⇒ d = 1.102. Independent confirmation: direct box-count slopes plateau at 1.07–1.10 in the fractal regime (L8: [...,1.080,1.097,1.092,1.093,1.147,...]). The d=1.10 value depends on the MEASURED λ; the first-principles derivation gives only the bracket (1,1.465]. Proving λ≈3.35 (mean ~2.35 interiors/edge, not the cap 4) is the one non-rigorous input to the exponent.
routes.C_derived: C is controlled by the bounded-distortion κ=1.323 and the arc/refill multiplicity, NOT by the exponent. The r^d law with C≈2 fits the SINGLE-LEVEL refill b_m(r)=t_0(r) — the object the birth-telescoping reduction (route 4) collapses the full count onto. Measured (L8): sup_r b_m(r)/r^1.10 = 4.0, binding only at r=1 (lattice granularity: b_m(1)=4 > 2); for r≥2 the ratio ≤2.8, decaying to 1.91 at r=10 → asymptotic C≈2, matching 2·10^1.10=25.2 vs measured b_m(10)=24. So C≈2 is the large-r/availability constant; a single clean constant on ALL of [1,10] needs C=4 (r=1 blemish) or the form C·r^d+F. The FULL worst-case crowding c_k needs C≈3.1 at the same d: direct fit c~3.14·r^0.97 (sup c/r^1.10 = 3.27 at L8), or via birth-telescope assembly c_k(r) ≤ 2·S'·r^1.10 + 1 = 3.06·r^1.10+1 with S'=1+(4/9)^1.10+(4/27)^1.10=1.532. Both are finite and level-uniform.
routes.key_sublemma: SINGLE-LEVEL ANISOTROPIC d-REGULARITY (arc-incidence). The sole residue: one level's fresh stitch interiors satisfy the anisotropic content bound b_m(ellipsoid E) ≤ C·σ₁(E)·σ₂(E)^{d-1}, equivalently: a Cheb-r ball meets O(r^{d-1})=O(r^{0.10}) distinct connector-arcs of a single level, each contributing O(r) points (proven diameter-ballistic sojourn ≤3.1r). Then b_m(r) ≤ C·r·r^{d-1} = C·r^d. Route 1 does NOT prove the arc-incidence exponent from first principles — it PROVIDES the machinery that consumes it: bounded distortion (proven) + the anisotropic pullback contraction φ=0.353<1 (proven) turn this per-level statement into c_k ≤ C·r^d uniform in k, WITHOUT the divergence (20/9>1) that killed every isotropic route. Measured arc-count exponent is ~0.33–0.39 (maxarcs~r^0.35), larger than the target 0.10 but with strong length/count anti-correlation (many arcs ⇒ each short), consistent with the b_m ≤ C·r^d envelope. This is the same irreducible curve-like "linear/sub-cubic-not-cubic" incidence fact all four routes reduce to; Route 1 reframes it in the sharp anisotropic (singular-value) metric where the induction provably closes.
routes.computational_evidence: Ran on real construction chains gate2-l7-construction-L{6,7,8}.pkl (27,697 / 92,732 / 311,738 pts), pypy3, single process. (A) LINEAR ALGEBRA: eigenvalue moduli all 3 (elliptic); invariant PD form Q=eig[2.0,2.5,3.5] solving MᵀQM=9Q ⇒ κ=√cond=1.323; σ_i(M^j)/3^j ∈ [0.846,1.182] over j=1..10 (bounded distortion CONFIRMED). σ(M)=[3.541,3.000,2.541], σ(M⁻¹)=[0.3935,0.3333,0.2824]. Anisotropic pullback φ=σ₁σ₂^{d-1}=0.3519 at d=1.102 (<1, CONTRACTS) vs isotropic-linear coeff 20/9=2.22 (>1, diverged). (B) MEASUREMENT: point-growth λ=3.348(L7)/3.362(L8), extent-ratio 3.0000 exact ⇒ d=log λ/log 3=1.100/1.104. Box-count slopes plateau 1.07–1.10 in the fractal regime. (C) LOCAL COUNT c_k(q,r) worst-case over walk centres, r=1..10: fits exponent 0.96/0.92/0.97 — i.e. essentially LINEAR on [1,10] (the r^1.10 is the r≫10 box dimension; on the fixed range the set is locally arc-like, fine-scale slope→1). sup c/r^1.10 = 3.27 (full crowding). (D) SINGLE-LEVEL refill t_0 matches 2·r^1.10 at availability radii: t_0(10)=24 vs 25.2, t_0(4.44)≈10.3 vs 10.3. All written to design/lemma/dim/route1_affinity_results.json.
routes.script: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/dim/route1_affinity.py — self-contained. Part A (hand-rolled LA, no numpy): eigenvalues, invariant PD form Q via null-space of MᵀQM−9Q, Jacobi singular values of M^j, distortion band κ, affinity-dimension pressure collapse, anisotropic-vs-isotropic pullback factor. Part B: builds L6/L7/L8 chains via gate_run.word_interiors, measures λ/extent/box-slopes, worst-case c_k(q,r) with arc-incidence, power-law fits. Run: PYTHONPATH=/Users/erik/homelab/math193 /Users/erik/homelab/tools/pypy3.11-v7.3.20-macos_x86_64/bin/pypy3 design/lemma/dim/route1_affinity.py (~60s). Output: design/lemma/dim/route1_affinity_results.json.
routes.gap: Two residuals, one shrunk and one shared. (1) THE EXPONENT rests on the MEASURED branching λ≈3.35: first-principles gives only 1<d≤log5/log3=1.465 (from ≤4 interiors/edge). d=1.10 needs λ≈3.35 proven (mean ~2.35 interiors/edge), which is a per-level combinatorial count of the actual construction, not derived here. At the loose cap d=1.465 the availability check fails (2·4.44^1.465=17.6>12), so the tight availability constant genuinely requires the measured λ. (2) THE UPPER BOUND c_k≤C·r^d reduces to the SAME single-level d-regularity/arc-incidence clean lemma as routes 2/3/4 — Route 1 does NOT prove it independently; its contribution is to (a) identify d=log λ/log 3 as the exponent via bounded distortion (rigorous given λ) and (b) supply the anisotropic singular-value pullback that makes the induction CONTRACT (φ=0.353<1) where the isotropic Cheb norm diverged (20/9>1). So the divergence obstruction that blocked every prior linear round is REMOVED, and the residue is sharpened to a single per-level anisotropic-content statement — but it remains an all-k regularity claim, not a finished finite check. Honesty note on constants: on the fixed range [1,10] the FULL local worst-case count is empirically LINEAR (~3.1r), so 2·r^1.10 is the availability-grade envelope for the single-level refill / birth-telescope term (matches at r=4.44 and r=10, ≤12 threshold), while the assembled full c_k needs C≈3.06 with the same d — availability-viable but not the optimistic C=2.

routes.route: ROUTE 2 — transfer operator / renewal on the EXACT birth-telescoping. Two independent pressure equations for the dimension d, closed by the measured per-shell charge kappa_j, plus telescoping amplitude S_d for the constant. Script: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/dim/route2_pressure.py; conclusion JSON: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/dim/route2-pressure-CONCLUSION.json.
routes.proof:
Exact input (verified elsewhere, re-checked): Walk_L = M·Walk_{L-1} ⊔ Stitch_{L-1}; interiors born at level m = word-interiors of the L=m construction pkl, decorating anchors(m)=M·Walk_{m-1}=pkl['anchors']; in the Walk_K frame a birth-(K-j) interior P appears as M^j·P (M integer), so its ball-membership is exact: P∈M^{-j}B(q,r) ⟺ cheb(M^j P, q)≤r. Telescoping c_k(q,ρ)=Σ_j t_j, t_j=#{interiors(K-j) ∩ M^{-j}B(q,ρ)} ≤ b(‖M^{-j}‖_∞·ρ), b = single-level refill.

STEP 1 (exponent, isotropic pressure). M is elliptic with all eigenvalue-moduli 3, so ‖M^{-j}‖_∞ = Θ(3^{-j}): measured ‖M^{-j}‖_∞·3^j = [1,1.33,1.33,1.52,1.52,1.54,...] stays in [1,1.54] (bounded distortion — provable from M diagonalizable over C with |λ|=3, distortion = eigenbasis condition number). Extent grows by det(M)^{1/3}=27^{1/3}=3 exactly (measured 3.00055→3.00018→3.00000). Point count grows by level-stable λ (measured 3.372→3.348→3.362). Pressure equation λ·3^{-d}=1 ⟹ d=log λ/log 3 = 1.1018. The singular-value anisotropy (σ=2.54/3/3.54) is a bounded transient absorbed into C, not d.

STEP 2 (transfer self-consistency, independent check). Single-level refill relates to the parent: b(r) ≤ κ̄·c((4/9)r)+O(1) (anchors=M·Walk_parent pull back by ‖M^{-1}‖=4/9). Substituting into telescoping gives the closed renewal κ̄·(4/9)^d·S_d=1, S_d=Σ_j‖M^{-j}‖_∞^d. With S_{1.10}=1.591 this solves κ̄=1.536; measured b(r)/c((4/9)r)≈1.37–2.0 (avg ~1.5). Same d=1.10 falls out — the two pressure equations agree.

STEP 3 (constant). Telescoping: if b(r)≤B·r^d then c(ρ)≤B·S_d·ρ^d with S_{1.10}=1.592. Residual is the single-level refill amplitude B.
routes.d_derived: d = log(λ)/log(3) = 1.1018 (target ≈1.10 confirmed). Derived TWO independent ways that agree: (a) isotropic pressure λ·3^{-d}=1 using λ = level-stable point-growth (measured 3.3549, avg L7/L8) and the exact extent-rate 3 = det(M)^{1/3} (M elliptic, all |eigenvalues|=3, giving ‖M^{-j}‖_∞=Θ(3^{-j}), bounded-distortion factor in [1,1.54]); (b) transfer-operator self-consistency κ̄·(4/9)^d·S_d=1 with measured κ̄≈1.5 and S_d=1.591. Rigor: d is EXACT modulo one clean lemma — λ equals the Perron eigenvalue of the finite stitch-incidence matrix (a computable algebraic number ≈3.35); measured λ is level-stable to 3 significant figures across L6/L7/L8, strong evidence it is that fixed Perron root.
routes.C_derived: C = B·S_d where S_d = Σ_j‖M^{-j}‖_∞^d = 1.592 at d=1.10 (1.677 at d=1, 1.589 at d=1.104) and B is the single-level refill amplitude (b(r)≤B·r^d). Honest values on [1,10]: for the FULL count c_k the minimal single-constant is C≈3.27 (walk-centred sup, binds at r=2) to 4.0 (all-integer-centre sup, binds at r=1) — NOT 2. The task's C≈2 correctly models only the SINGLE-LEVEL REFILL b (b/r^1.10: 3.0 at r=1 decaying to 1.6 at r=10; 2·r^1.10 tracks b at large r), not the full c_k. With B≈3 (refill amplitude at its r=1 binding), C=B·S_d≈4.8 as a telescoping upper bound; the tight measured full-c constant is ≈2.5 at large r.
routes.key_sublemma: ANTI-STACKING via the per-shell charge κ_j = (interiors donated per birth-(K-j) anchor to a Cheb-r ball). MEASURED (1500 sampled centres, K7 and K8 near-identical = level-stable): κ_j is O(1), bounded by ~2.1 with max ever 3 — NOT the crude menu multiplicity g=4. Deep shells j≥2 give κ_j=1.0 EXACTLY; the freshest shell κ_0 grows only from 1.0 (r=1) to 2.14 (r=10); κ_1 from 1.0 to 1.84. This bounded, level-stable charge is exactly what pins d at 1.10 (it is the κ̄≈1.5 that closes the transfer pressure equation) and collapses the tail. It reduces the standalone cubic packing bound (O(r^3)) to the curve-like O(r^d) incidence. Residual clean lemma = single-level refill power law b(r)≤B·r^d (the arc-incidence / curve fact: a Cheb-r ball meets O(r^d), not O(r^3), connector-arcs of one level) — the SAME residue isolated by route4.
routes.script: /Users/erik/homelab/math193/design/lemma/dim/route2_pressure.py (run: PYTHONPATH=/Users/erik/homelab/math193 /Users/erik/homelab/tools/pypy3.11-v7.3.20-macos_x86_64/bin/pypy3 design/lemma/dim/route2_pressure.py). Raw output: design/lemma/dim/route2_pressure-results.json. Conclusion: design/lemma/dim/route2-pressure-CONCLUSION.json. Builds Walk_L and birth-shells from gate2-l7-construction-L{5,6,7,8}.pkl via word-interiors, dilates each shell by M^j into the Walk_K frame (exact integer), grid-buckets, and measures t_j / distinct-donating-anchors = κ_j plus λ, ‖M^{-j}‖_∞, S_d, c_full and b.
routes.gap:
Two residues (both finite/checkable, non-circular). (1) DIMENSION: prove λ = Perron eigenvalue of the finite stitch-incidence matrix so d=log λ/log 3 is exact (measured level-stable to 3 digits, ≈3.3549 → d=1.1018); plus the bounded-distortion ‖M^{-j}‖_∞≤κ_M·3^{-j} (provable, κ_M≈1.54 = eigenbasis condition number). (2) CONSTANT: the single-level refill power law b(r)≤B·r^d (arc-incidence: a Cheb-r ball meets O(r^d) not O(r^3) connector-arcs) — the same clean lemma route4 isolates; anti-stacking (measured κ_j≤2.1, →1 for deep shells) is strong evidence but not a proof.

HONEST OVERSHOOT ON THE HEADLINE CLAIM: the task's C≈2 is NOT availability-grade for the full count. (a) 2·r^1.10 undershoots measured full c_k (gives 10.3 at r=4.44 vs c≈12–14, and 25 at r=10 vs c=31) — it actually models the single-level refill b, not c. (b) The minimal single power-law constant that bounds full c_k on [1,10] is C≈3.3–4.0, and 3.3·4.44^1.10≈17 > 12, so it BLOWS THE 12-GATE. No single (C,d) power law simultaneously bounds the measured full count on [1,10] AND stays ≤12 at r=4.44 — hence availability_grade=false for a bare power law. Availability is genuinely deliverable, but through the exact 3-term telescoping c_k ≤ t_0(ρ)+t_1(4ρ/9)+t_2(4ρ/27)+1 (each term an r^1.10 law with κ_j≈1, deep tail ≤1 by min|M^3 v|=24>2r), not through c ≤ C·r^d with C=2.

routes.route: ROUTE 3 — DIRECT BOX-COVERING INDUCTION (self-affine covering-number induction) for c_k(q,r) ≤ C·r^d on r∈[1,10], uniform in k, d=log λ/log 3. Implemented and measured at L5/L6/L7/L8 in design/lemma/dim/route3_covering.py.
routes.proof: Route 3 rigorously supplies the EXPONENT and the covering-to-count conversion, but does NOT deliver the availability-grade constant. What is rigorous: (1) EXPONENT. The exact recursion W_{k+1}=M·W_k ⊔ Stitch_k gives extent-ratio EXACTLY 3 = det(M)^{1/3} (M elliptic, det 27, all eigenvalue moduli 3; measured 3.0005/3.0002/3.0000) and point-growth λ level-stable at 3.372/3.348/3.362, so d = log λ/log 3 = 1.1036 is pinned. (2) COVERING MONOTONICITY. M·W_k ⊆ W_{k+1} as a cell-subset at every tested scale (side 3,6,9; VERIFIED True at L6/L7/L8), so a side-r cover of W_k pushes forward to a side-3r cover of the M-image and the stitch only ADDS boxes — the induction has the right shape. (3) CONVERSION. All walk points occupy distinct unit cells (min|Mv|_∞=3 separation of anchors) and a Cheb-1 ball holds ≤3 points, so covering-number → point-count is an O(1) conversion. WHERE IT BREAKS (two independent failures, both measured): (a) the isotropic box-distortion of M is NOT O(1) — κ_M = #{side-3r cells covering M·(side-r cell)} measures 1,2,3 at r=1,2,3, i.e. grows with scale, because ‖M‖_∞=4 > 3=det^{1/3}; cube-covering leaks the anisotropy factor, so the induction does not carry the λ-per-3×-scale multiplier with a uniform constant Q. (b) the availability window r∈[1,10] is PRE-ASYMPTOTIC: the per-3×-scale box ratio N_b/N_{3b} climbs 1.12→2.16→2.99→3.27 and only reaches λ≈3.36 at b≥27, so C·r^{1.10} does not model the local count in [1,10] at all. Consequently the smallest VALID C over the whole range is 3.257 (binding at L8, r=2, forced by small-r discreteness), which OVERSHOOTS to C·4.44^d = 16.88 at the load-bearing radius — far above the threshold 12. The task's premise C≈2 is REFUTED: 2·4.44^1.104 = 10.36 is not a valid upper bound because the measured value there is 12, not ~10.
routes.d_derived: d = 1.1036 = log(λ)/log(3), with λ = N_{k+1}/N_k the point-growth ratio (measured level-stable: 3.3719, 3.3481, 3.3617 at L6/L7/L8) and the denominator log 3 because the Chebyshev extent-ratio is EXACTLY 3 = det(M)^{1/3}. The extent-ratio-3 half is rigorous: M_BAL3=((3,0,0),(0,0,-3),(0,3,-1)) is elliptic with det 27 and all three eigenvalue moduli equal to 3 (2×2 block char poly λ²+λ+9), so each level scales linear extent by exactly 3 (measured 3.0005→3.0002→3.0000, converging to the exact value). The λ half is empirical/level-stable, not yet a theorem (it is the point-growth of the bounded-menu stitch, which is what the residual lemma controls). So d is proven up to proving λ converges.
routes.C_derived: The smallest C making c_k(q,r) ≤ C·r^d a VALID upper bound on r∈[1,10] across all measured levels is C = 3.257 (binding at L8, r=2: c=7, 7/2^1.104=3.257); at r=1, C=3.0. This is set by small-r lattice discreteness, not by the fractal regime. It is NOT availability-grade: C·r^d at r=4.44 = 3.257·5.18 = 16.88 ≫ 12. The task's suggested C≈2 is not valid (2·4.44^1.104 = 10.36 < measured 12, so it under-bounds). There is NO single constant that is simultaneously a valid upper bound at r=1 (needs C≥3) and ≤12 at r=4.44 (needs C≤2.32) — the pure power-law framing is internally inconsistent for this window.
routes.key_sublemma: The ANTI-STACKING / ARC-INCIDENCE lemma (identical to the residue isolated by Routes 2/4 and the birth-telescoping analysis): a Chebyshev-r ball meets O(r) connector-arcs of a single level (the walk is curve-like, not cubically packed), so the single-level refill count is linear rather than the cubic 3-separated-packing bound. Route 3 confirms the two structural ingredients this lemma feeds (covering monotonicity M·W_k⊆W_{k+1}, and O(1) points-per-box conversion) but does NOT prove it. IMPORTANT sharpening from this route: even granting the lemma in covering/power-law form (covering-count ≤ Q·(E/r)^d) does NOT close availability, because the constant overshoots at r=4.44; availability additionally requires the strictly stronger TIGHT DISTRIBUTION-INVARIANCE statement — that the measured load-bearing c(q,4) stays ≤12 across all levels — which the anti-stacking lemma alone does not deliver.
routes.computational_evidence: Measured at L5/L6/L7/L8 on the verified gate2 walks (exact integer arithmetic). (A) λ=3.3719/3.3481/3.3617, extent-ratio=3.0005/3.0002/3.0000 → d=1.1064/1.0999/1.1036. (B) Induction step: M·W_k ⊆ W_{k+1} as side-{3,6,9} cells VERIFIED True at all levels; isotropic box-distortion κ_M=1,2,3 at r=1,2,3 (grows — anisotropy leak, ‖M‖_∞=4>3); per-3×-scale box ratio N_b/N_{3b}=1.12,2.16,2.99,3.27,3.34,3.32 (reaches λ only at b≥27, so [1,10] is pre-asymptotic). (C) Conversion: all points distinct in unit cells; c(q,1)=3 pts/Cheb-1 ball. (D) Direct fit: valid C over [1,10] = 3.257 (binding L8, r=2); C·4.44^d=16.88; C=2 gives 10.36 which is NOT a valid upper bound. AVAILABILITY (model-independent, the decisive fact): measured load-bearing c(q,4.44)=c(q,4) [Chebyshev distances are integers so Cheb≤4.44 ⟺ Cheb≤4] = 10,10,11,12 at L5/L6/L7/L8 — it already EQUALS the threshold 12 at L8 with ZERO margin and is creeping upward. No functional bound (power-law or affine) can be both valid and <12 at r=4.44 once the measured value there is 12.
routes.script: design/lemma/dim/route3_covering.py (pypy3, PYTHONPATH=/Users/erik/homelab/math193) → design/lemma/dim/route3-covering-results.json; verdict at design/lemma/dim/route3-VERDICT.json. Run: PYTHONPATH=. pypy3 design/lemma/dim/route3_covering.py
routes.gap: Two gaps, one fatal to the stated target. (1) SALVAGEABLE: the covering-number induction leaks the anisotropy of M — isotropic cube-distortion κ_M grows with scale (1,2,3) because ‖M‖_∞=4 exceeds det(M)^{1/3}=3, so the induction does not carry the λ-per-3×-scale multiplier with a uniform constant. Fixing this needs the ANISOTROPIC (singular-value/ellipsoid) covering, which is exactly the anti-stacking content — Route 3 does not escape it, it repackages it. (2) FATAL to availability-grade: the availability window r∈[1,10] lies BELOW the fractal regime (per-3×-scale box ratio reaches λ only at b≥27), so c_k in [1,10] is not governed by any single-constant C·r^{1.10}; a valid C (3.26) overshoots to 16.9 at r=4.44. More fundamentally, the measured load-bearing count c(q,4.44)=c(q,4)=12 already equals the threshold at L8 with ZERO margin and a rising trend — so the availability claim in the task premise (C≈2 ⇒ 10.4 ≤ 12) is refuted; the true measured value is 12. Availability is therefore not deliverable by ANY bound of covering-dimension type; it requires proving the tight distribution-invariance (c(q,4) stays ≤12 uniformly in k), a strictly stronger statement than the box-dimension/anti-stacking lemma. Route 3's positive contribution: it pins the exponent d=1.104 (extent-ratio exactly 3 is rigorous), establishes covering monotonicity M·W_k⊆W_{k+1}, and gives the O(1) points-per-box conversion — reducing no-blow-up (already known) to the same arc-incidence lemma, but it does not close availability even modulo that lemma.

verdict.synthesis:
STRONGEST HONEST SYNTHESIS. The fractal reframing makes two GENUINE advances but does NOT close the lemma at availability grade, and the residue is the same anti-stacking fact every prior route died on — now renamed, not proven.

WHAT IS RIGOROUS (real, new): (1) Extent-ratio = 3 = det(M)^{1/3} EXACTLY. M=((3,0,0),(0,0,-3),(0,3,-1)) is elliptic: coord-0 eigenvalue 3, 2x2 block char poly x^2+x+9 gives moduli sqrt(9)=3; all three eigenvalue moduli equal 3, M diagonalizable. Measured 3.0005/3.0002/3.0000. This half of d is a theorem. (2) BOUNDED DISTORTION: the invariant PD form Q with M^T Q M = 9Q (eig[2.0,2.5,3.5]) gives kappa=sqrt(cond Q)=1.323; measured sigma_i(M^j)/3^j in [0.846,1.182] over j=1..10. So M is quasi-self-SIMILAR (ratio 3), distortion absorbed into constants. (3) THE DECISIVE UNBLOCK: the isotropic Chebyshev pullback gave coefficient ||M^-1||_inf(1+g)=20/9>1 (every prior induction DIVERGED); the anisotropic singular-value pullback phi = sigma_1(M^-1)*sigma_2(M^-1)^{d-1} = 0.393*0.333^{0.10} = 0.353 < 1 CONTRACTS. This is a real, rigorous improvement: the induction finally has the right shape. (4) Deep-tail collapse (min|M^3 v|_inf=24>2r for r<=10 ⇒ shells j>=3 contribute <=1) and the exact 3-term telescoping c_k = t_0(r)+t_1(4r/9)+t_2(4r/27)+O(1) are rigorous.

WHAT IS NOT PROVEN (the residue): (a) The EXPONENT rests on MEASURED lambda≈3.35. First-principles gives only the bracket 1 < d <= log5/log3 = 1.465 (from <=4 interiors/edge). d=1.10 needs lambda = Perron root of the finite stitch-incidence matrix PROVEN; it is level-stable to 3 digits but is not a theorem. At the loose cap d=1.465 availability fails outright (2*4.44^1.465=17.6). (b) ANTI-STACKING is RENAMED, not proven. The 'single-level anisotropic d-regularity / arc-incidence' lemma (a Cheb-r ball meets O(r^{d-1}) arcs, each O(r) points ⇒ b(r)<=C r^d) is the SAME residue Routes 2/3/4 isolate. Route 1 supplies the metric that WOULD consume it (phi<1) but does not prove the per-level statement. The per-shell charge kappa_j is MEASURED (avg ~1-2.1, deep shells exactly 1) — strong evidence but explicitly not a proof, and note kappa_MAX=3 appears even in 1500 sampled centres while shell-0 t_max=13 at r=10: a worst-case bound (which the lemma needs) is not the measured average.

THE ADVERSARIAL KILL (model-independent, confirmed by all three routes' own data): take q = the L8 walk point realizing the sup. A Cheb-4 ball around it contains 12 level-8 points. Because Chebyshev distance is integer-valued, this ball IS the Cheb-4.44 ball, so c_k(q,4.44)=12 = the threshold, with ZERO margin, and the trend is monotone rising 10→10→11→12 (L5-L8). Consequences: (i) C=2, d=1.10 gives 2*4.44^1.10 = 10.36 < 12 = measured ⇒ C≈2 is NOT an upper bound on the full count; it silently models the single-level refill b (b(4.44)=9), not c_k. Route 1's own fit_L8 already yields C_for_d110=3.27, contradicting its headline availability_grade=true. (ii) The smallest C making C r^1.10 a VALID upper bound on measured c_k over [1,10] is 3.27 (binds L8 r=2) ⇒ 3.27*4.44^1.10 = 16.9 >> 12. (iii) No fixed (C,d) power law is simultaneously a valid upper bound AND <=12 at r=4.44 — the framing is internally inconsistent on this window. Even the telescoping 'how availability is met' RECONSTRUCTS exactly 12 at L8 with the same rising trend. Since local count for fixed r plateaus (shells j<=2 mature by L8, j>=3 collapse), the true plateau c_inf(q,4) is ≈12-13 — at or over the threshold. Proving the arc-incidence lemma does NOT fix this: availability is a CONSTANT/tight-distribution-invariance question, not an exponent question.

BOTTOM LINE: the qualitative/exponent sub-target (c_k <= C r^d for SOME finite C, no-blow-up refined to d≈1.10) CLOSES-MODULO-CLEAN-LEMMA (arc-incidence + lambda=Perron). The stated AVAILABILITY-GRADE target (C≈2, <=12 at 4.44) is STUCK/refuted: measured value already equals the threshold with zero margin and rises, and C≈2 is not an upper bound. This is the same anti-stacking residue, now sharpened and re-metricized but not discharged. I am not manufacturing a close.
verdict.antistacking_proven_or_renamed: RENAMED, not proven. The fractal framing recasts anti-stacking as 'single-level anisotropic d-regularity / arc-incidence' (a Cheb-r ball meets O(r^{d-1}) connector-arcs) and — genuinely new — supplies the anisotropic singular-value pullback phi=sigma_1(M^-1)*sigma_2(M^-1)^{d-1}=0.353<1 in which the induction CONTRACTS (removing the 20/9>1 divergence that killed every isotropic route). But it does NOT prove the per-level statement. The per-shell charge kappa_j is MEASURED (avg 1.0-2.14, deep shells j>=2 exactly 1.0, level-stable K7~K8), which is strong evidence — but kappa_max=3 is observed even in 1500 sampled centres and shell-0 t_max hits 13 at r=10, so the worst-case charge the lemma actually requires is bounded by ~3, not ~1, and is not proven for all k. Same residue Routes 2/3/4 die on.
verdict.best_C_d: d = 1.10 = log(lambda)/log(3): the log-3 (extent=det^{1/3}) half is RIGOROUS; the lambda≈3.355 half is MEASURED (level-stable 3 digits), rigorous only modulo lambda=Perron root of the stitch-incidence matrix. First-principles rigorous bracket: 1 < d <= log5/log3 = 1.465. For a genuine UPPER bound on the measured full count c_k over r in [1,10] at d=1.10, the smallest valid constant is C ≈ 3.27 (binds L8 at r=2) — but this is a fit over 4 levels, not proven uniform in k, and it gives 16.9 at r=4.44 (blows the 12-gate). C≈2 is REFUTED (2*4.44^1.10=10.4 < measured 12; it bounds only the single-level refill b, not c_k). No fixed (C,d) power law is both a valid upper bound and <=12 at r=4.44. Rigorous availability-grade (C,d): NONE exists.
verdict.next: Stop chasing the box-dimension exponent (it is not the binding constraint) and attack availability as a FINITE CHECK, which the exact structure now permits. Because (1) the stitch interiors come from a FIXED FINITE MENU, (2) anchors M*Z^3 are Chebyshev->=3 separated, (3) the birth-telescoping is exact with c_k(q,r) = t_0(r)+t_1(4r/9)+t_2(4r/27)+O(1), and (4) shells j>=3 collapse to <=1 (min|M^3 v|=24>2r), the worst-case plateau c_inf(q,r) is a MAXIMUM OVER A FINITE SET of local anchor+menu configurations — exactly computable, not an asymptotic regularity claim. Concretely: (a) enumerate the finite local stitch configurations and compute the exact sup of t_0(4), t_1(cheb 1), t_2 over them; if that finite max is <=12 you get a RIGOROUS availability bound with an honest margin; if it is 13+, the availability premise at radius 4.44 / threshold 12 is FALSE and the downstream must renegotiate (reduce the load-bearing radius below 4, or raise the threshold). (b) Independently, prove lambda = Perron eigenvalue of the finite stitch-incidence matrix to upgrade d=1.10 from measured to exact. The measured trend c(q,4)=10,10,11,12 rising suggests the finite max is a photo-finish at 12-13 — so run the exact finite enumeration BEFORE claiming availability; it is the only route that yields a rigorous margin, and it may well show the current threshold is unmet.
